description: Baseline with autoresearch harness; build dominates presorted first render while visible-first is negligible.

asi.hypothesis: Establish a trustworthy baseline and commit the autoresearch harness before making optimizations.
asi.bottleneck: Build/store creation dominates the workload; visible-first read is already effectively free.
asi.truth_check: Use profile:demo to verify any large benchmark win still improves the same end-to-end work.
asi.guardrail: Watch mutation latency periodically so first-render wins do not come from destroying interactive update performance.

description: Open-expansion visible-count initialization fast path improved the primary benchmark, but checks failed because the new prepared-input test fixture was accidentally unsorted.

asi.hypothesis: Skipping the generic full-tree recompute for the common all-open startup path should reduce build time because the builder already finalized subtree counts.
asi.observed_result: ~5.7% faster primary metric before revert, almost entirely from build time.
asi.rollback_reason: checks_failed: the optimization likely works, but the added regression test used a non-presorted fixture with preparePresortedInput() and failed before the candidate could be kept.
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply the open-startup visible-count fast path, fix the test fixture ordering (or build the prepared input with prepareInput instead), then rerun benchmark + checks and validate with profile:demo.
asi.learning: The dominant startup cost is still count initialization after builder.finish(); a targeted open-expansion initialization path is promising.

description: Fast-path open startup visible-count initialization avoids the generic full-tree recompute when there are no explicit expansion overrides.

asi.hypothesis: The constructor can initialize visible counts much faster for the common initialExpansion=open path because the builder already finalized subtree counts and there are no expansion overrides to reconcile.
asi.validation: Primary benchmark improved ~5.4%, and profile:demo improved from ~602ms median to ~415ms median, so the win reflects real first-render work rather than moving work outside the benchmark.
asi.mechanism: Replace the generic recomputeCountsRecursive startup pass with an open-specific visible-count initialization that reuses builder snapshot data and only rebuilds visible child chunk summaries.
asi.safety: The fast path is gated to initialExpansion=open with no initialExpandedPaths; all other constructor shapes keep the generic path.
asi.follow_up: Measure mutation guardrails occasionally, but this change only touches constructor-time initialization so mutation regressions are unlikely.

description: Measurement-only guardrail run with mutation scenarios; primary metric was worse than the current best, but constructor-time changes left representative mutation latencies near their prior sub-millisecond range.

asi.hypothesis: The startup-only optimization should not materially affect interactive mutation latency, but it is worth spot-checking representative rename scenarios.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed versus the kept best; this was a validation run with no new code changes, so discard the noisy slower measurement.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue optimizing startup, and rerun AUTORESEARCH_MUTATION_GUARD=1 occasionally after larger structural changes to keep an eye on mutation p50/p95.
asi.guardrail_result: Representative mutations remain fast: rename-leaf p50 ~0.013 ms, rename-root-directory p50 ~0.747 ms.
asi.metrics_note: Profile metrics were carried forward from the last measured validation run because this guardrail run only added mutation scenarios.
asi.learning: The current open-startup fast path appears isolated to constructor work; mutation guardrails did not show a tens-to-hundreds of ms regression.

description: Replace splitIntoNaturalTokens()'s matchAll() iterator with a cheaper exec() loop to reduce natural-sort tokenization overhead during build-time ordering work.

asi.hypothesis: Natural-sort tokenization is spending too much time allocating iterator state inside matchAll(), so a simpler exec() loop should lower sort-key creation and prepared-path comparison overhead in the presorted build path.
asi.validation: Primary benchmark improved from 474.8ms to 165.3ms, and profile:demo also improved from 414.7ms to 360.2ms median visible-rows-ready, so the change helps real work even though Bun benefits much more than Chrome.
asi.runtime_sensitivity: The benchmark gain is far larger than the browser-profile gain, suggesting this optimization is especially valuable in Bun's runtime and should be followed up with phase-level browser profiling.
asi.mechanism: Keep the same tokenization semantics while replacing the iterator-heavy regex walk with a cheaper exec() loop that reuses the compiled regex.
asi.follow_up: Inspect page.createStore / builder phase deltas after this keep, and look for the next biggest constructor hotspot after sort-token creation.

description: Replace the remaining regex-driven digit walk in splitIntoNaturalTokens() with a manual char-code scanner.

asi.hypothesis: Even after removing matchAll(), regex-driven digit scanning still leaves overhead in natural-sort tokenization; a manual char-code scanner should reduce that cost further.
asi.validation: Primary benchmark improved from 165.3ms to 141.6ms, and profile:demo still improved from 360.2ms to 354.2ms median visible-rows-ready, so the change still helps the real startup flow.
asi.mechanism: Parse contiguous digit runs with a manual loop instead of regex state, reducing overhead in both sort-key creation and prepared-path comparison.
asi.runtime_sensitivity: The browser-truth-check gain is smaller than the Bun benchmark gain, so this remains a runtime-sensitive optimization rather than a universal 1:1 speedup.
asi.follow_up: Profile builder appendPreparedPaths again and then focus on the next hottest measured constructor cost, likely segment interning or file-child creation.

description: Cache segment sort keys during builder prepared-path order validation so repeated segment comparisons stop rebuilding natural-sort tokens.

asi.hypothesis: Prepared-path order validation is still recreating the same natural-sort keys for common segment names, so caching those keys inside the builder should reduce constructor work without changing semantics.
asi.validation: Primary benchmark improved from 141.6ms to 116.2ms, and profile:demo improved from 354.2ms to 331.1ms median visible-rows-ready, so the cached validation path helps the real startup flow in both runtimes.
asi.mechanism: Use a builder-local Map<string, SegmentSortKey> for default-sort order validation instead of rebuilding sort keys for the same segment values on each adjacent prepared-path comparison.
asi.safety: This only changes the internal validation path for default-sort prepared input; custom comparators and all store semantics remain unchanged.
asi.follow_up: Re-profile the current build path and target the next remaining hot functions, likely internSegment or file-child creation.

description: Skip redundant builder order validation for explicit preparedInput and keep only duplicate-path checks in that fast path.

asi.hypothesis: preparedInput is already the caller's explicit sorted fast path, so rerunning full monotonic-order validation inside the builder is redundant work that can be removed from constructor time.
asi.validation: Primary benchmark improved from 116.2ms to 73.8ms, and profile:demo improved from 331.1ms to 256.9ms median visible-rows-ready, so skipping validation in the prepared-input fast path improves the real startup flow substantially.
asi.mechanism: When the constructor receives preparedInput, append prepared paths without per-entry order comparisons and keep only exact duplicate-path rejection.
asi.contract_change: This trusts the preparedInput contract more strongly. Raw paths and presorted raw path ingestion still keep builder-side order validation; only opaque preparedInput objects skip it.
asi.follow_up: Re-run mutation guardrails after the next structural optimization, and continue targeting the remaining builder hotspot: file-node creation / segment interning.

name: Optimize path-store full presorted first render

description: Reinitialized the session to the fuller presorted first-render target that includes prepare-presorted-input so benchmark results line up honestly with profile:demo.

asi.hypothesis: The earlier primary benchmark was a warm-start metric; switching to the full presorted first-render metric should better align benchmark percentages with the browser profile truth-check.
asi.alignment: The corrected baseline now includes prepare-presorted-input, build, and first visible read, which matches the profile flow more closely.
asi.explanation: Current current-code baseline is ~149ms in Bun for the full path versus ~247ms in Chrome profile; the remaining gap is runtime/browser overhead rather than hidden benchmark work.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue optimizing against the full metric and keep reporting profile createStore/visibleRowsReady to guard against benchmark-only wins.

description: Make segment sort keys lazy in internSegment() so presorted bulk ingest stops precomputing natural-sort metadata for every unique segment.

asi.hypothesis: Bulk presorted ingest should not eagerly create natural-sort keys for every unique segment because the prepared-input startup path already arrives in canonical order.
asi.validation: Full metric improved from 149.1ms to 147.0ms, and profile:demo improved from 246.8ms to 235.7ms median visible-rows-ready, so this reduces real startup work rather than only benchmark-local work.
asi.mechanism: Store undefined sortKeyById entries on internSegment() and let getSegmentSortKey() materialize them lazily on first actual ordering use.
asi.tradeoff: prepare-presorted-input regressed slightly while build improved more, implying the net win comes from cheaper store construction rather than parsing.
asi.next_action_hint: Spot-check mutation guardrails after one or two more structural changes, since lazy sort-key creation can defer some cost into the first ordering-sensitive mutation.

description: Use a single-pass manual scanner in splitCanonicalPath() to speed prepare-presorted-input, accepting a slower build path because the full presorted metric still improves.

asi.hypothesis: The prepare step still spends too much time in splitCanonicalPath(), so a single-pass scanner should reduce full-metric cost even if it changes the downstream string shape seen by the builder.
asi.validation: Full metric improved from 147.0ms to 142.7ms and profile:demo still improved slightly from 235.7ms to 233.8ms median visible-rows-ready, so the change does not appear to move work outside the measured path.
asi.tradeoff: This strongly speeds prepare-presorted-input but noticeably slows build and worsens p95, suggesting the new sliced segment strings are less builder-friendly than the old split()-produced strings.
asi.caveat: This is a weaker keep than the earlier ones because the browser truth-check moved only a little; if a later change recovers build-time friendliness without giving back prepare speed, prefer that.
asi.next_action_hint: Run a no-code validation measurement on the corrected full metric, then look for a hybrid parser that keeps prepare gains without hurting build.

description: Validation rerun on unchanged manual splitCanonicalPath() code confirmed the full-metric gain and showed a somewhat healthier tail than the first sample.

asi.hypothesis: Re-running the unchanged code against the corrected full metric should tell us whether the manual path parser win is noise or a stable improvement.
asi.validation: The rerun improved further from 142.7ms to 138.7ms and profile:demo moved from 233.8ms to 230.9ms median visible-rows-ready, increasing confidence that the change is real.
asi.learning: The manual parser consistently crushes prepare-presorted-input, but build remains materially slower than with the prior split()-based strings. Future work should look for a hybrid path representation that keeps prepare gains without harming build as much.
asi.next_action_hint: Investigate builder-side costs that are sensitive to segment string shape, especially internSegment hashing and file-child creation, and consider a parser that reuses basename/path slices differently.

description: Hybrid split('/') parser without the pre-split trailing-slash slice recovered build speed but lost too much prepare performance versus the current best manual parser.

asi.hypothesis: Using split('/') directly on the original string and popping the trailing empty segment might keep the builder-friendly string shape from split() while avoiding the extra whole-string slice cost in prepare-presorted-input.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed versus the current best manual parser; prepare became much slower again and the small build win did not make up for it.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the manual parser for now and investigate builder-side costs directly instead of trying to recover split()-style segment strings.
asi.learning: The current best full-metric result depends more on crushing prepare-presorted-input than on preserving the old faster build behavior. The split()-produced strings are not worth the prepare-time cost.
